column1: Revenue & Receivables — Audit Risk Matrix (template). Hypothetical SME; based on the Standards on Auditing. L/I = Likelihood/Impact (H/M/L).

column3: Fictitious sales recorded to inflate revenue (PRESUMED FRAUD RISK)
column6: Dispatch precedes invoice; segregation between recording and dispatch
column7: Vouch sample invoices to delivery challan + e-way bill + customer acknowledgement; analytical review of sales vs dispatch volume

column3: Sales booked in the wrong period around year-end
column6: Invoicing driven by actual dispatch date
column7: Examine last/first ~15 dispatches and invoices around 31 March; trace to dispatch/GRN dates

column3: Goods dispatched but not invoiced / sales unrecorded
column6: Pre-numbered sequential dispatch documents reconciled to invoices
column7: Trace dispatch challans to sales invoices; test numerical sequence for gaps
Recompute a sample of invoices; agree prices to approved list and GST rates

column3: Channel stuffing or related-party sales reversed after year-end
column7: Review related-party sales and post-year-end sales returns; confirm terms

column3: Recorded trade receivables do not exist
column7: External confirmations (positive) for a sample; for non-replies use subsequent receipts and vouch to invoice + dispatch
Ageing analysis; review old and disputed balances; assess provision adequacy; check subsequent collections

column3: Non-trade or other income shown as revenue from operations
column7: Scan revenue ledger for unusual items; reclassify as needed

column3: Goods sold on consignment / bill-and-hold booked as sales
column7: Inspect terms for consignment or bill-and-hold; confirm risk-and-reward transfer (Ind AS 115 / AS 9)

column2: For revenue start from skepticism: real sales have a real customer
column4: and a real subsequent collection. Test all three.
